description: Wide flea-market scene filling the whole square: the dealer stands behind his stall making his very first opening argument of the day, one hand raised in a reserved oratorical gesture, the other resting on a small gilded mantel clock; one hesitant customer in a green coat listens, arms crossed. Modest half-empty stall, market street and neighbouring stands stretching to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and sparse — pale cool early-morning light, few props, quiet understated mood.

Same stall, same dealer, now in full verbal flight: leaning over the counter, both hands sketching an irresistible argument in the air, confident smile under the moustache; the customer leans in, half-won, hand hovering over his coat pocket, while a curious onlooker slows down behind him. Richer stall display, busier market street to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly richer than step 1 — warm late-afternoon light, more props and movement, confident mood.

The masterstroke: deal sealed with panache — the dealer and an elegant customer shake hands vigorously above the stall, the customer beaming and lifting his hat, a tall stack of coins and the gilded clock between them, two or three delighted bystanders applauding, bunting fluttering over the packed stall, the market in full swing to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: golden triumphant sunset light flooding the scene, abundance, dynamic composition at its most spectacular.

The dealer alone puts the finishing touch to a small, impeccably tidy stall — straightening a framed print between a polished vase and a neat row of teacups — while a single passer-by with an umbrella slows down, intrigued; the empty market street at dawn stretches to the edges of the square. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and sparse — pale cool early-morning light, few props, quiet understated mood.

The same stall now draws people in: the dealer welcomes with open arms a small delighted group — a lady with a parasol, an old gentleman on a cane, a young man in a flat cap — the display generous and layered, other strollers visible along the market street to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly richer than step 1 — warm afternoon light, more props and movement, confident mood.

The famous stand: a dense joyful crowd presses around the stall decked with bunting garlands, the dealer at its center holding court like a showman, arms wide; a wealthy client in top hat and fur-collared coat steps forward with his cane, children on tiptoe, the whole market square packed with life to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: golden festive light, abundance, dynamic bustling composition at its most spectacular.

Close two-figure scene filling the square: the dealer, chin resting thoughtfully on his hand, studies with a kindly attentive gaze the expressive face of a single customer absorbed in examining a small object; the stall interior and hanging wares fill the background to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and subtle — pale cool morning light, restrained gestures, quiet observational mood.

The dealer now reads deeper: glancing knowingly over small round spectacles at the coin purse a lady customer half-opens while she examines a painted teapot; her purse catches a distinctly warm highlight, the busy stall counter and shelves of wares filling the frame to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly sharper than step 1 — warm light, more customers browsing behind, confident appraising mood.

Total mastery of reading people: the dealer raises a gleaming monocle toward a customer and sees him entirely — the customer rendered in lighter, almost transparent linework like glass, while the coin purse at his chest is drawn precisely and glows warm gold as if seen through the coat; rays of insight radiate subtly from the monocle, the crowded stall dressed to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: dramatic golden light, almost mystical clairvoyance, the composition at its most striking.

Early morning café terrace filling the square: the dealer sits at a small round table reading a large opened newspaper whose print is pure abstract squiggle (strictly no letters), steaming coffee beside him; above the paper float two delicate ink drawings — a small sun and a small rain cloud — like a forecast materializing; the sleepy street and shopfronts extend to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and quiet — pale cool dawn light, sparse props, studious mood.

The dealer, half-hidden at a street corner, jots a note in a tiny pocket notebook while across the street an elegant celebrity in a long coat and feathered hat steps out of a gleaming 1920s motorcar under a striped awning; small ink stars of fascination hover around distant onlookers, the lively boulevard filling the frame to every edge. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly livelier than step 1 — warm golden-hour light, more figures and movement, worldly confident mood.

Master of the forecast itself: standing over his café table like a magician, sleeves rolled, the dealer rearranges the weather at will — in one raised hand a glowing card bearing a bright sun drawing, in the other a card bearing a rain cloud, more symbol-cards fanned on the open newspaper below, loose pages swirling in the wind around him; the café terrace and dramatic sky fill the square to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: theatrical golden light breaking through clouds, commanding pose, the composition at its most spectacular.

Bare workshop filling the square: at a simple workbench the dealer in a canvas apron carefully re-glues the loose leg of a small wooden chair, brow furrowed, a glue pot and two clamps beside him; the plain plank wall behind holds only three hanging tools, wood shavings on the floor to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and sparse — pale cool window light, few tools, humble apprentice mood.

The workshop has grown: at a better-equipped bench the dealer polishes the brass case of a mantel clock with a soft cloth, an oil can and a neat row of fine screwdrivers laid out, one side of the clock already gleaming; more tools on the wall, shelves with jars and parts filling the background to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly richer than step 1 — warm focused lamplight, more equipment, assured craftsman mood.

The master's atelier in its full glory: under an articulated brass lamp the dealer puts the final gilding touch to a magnificent vase, standing magnifier at hand; behind him an entire wall covered in finely ordered tools, and a long shelf where a row of freshly restored objects — clock, lamp, candlesticks — gleam like new, the workshop dense with treasures to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: rich golden workshop light, abundance of gleaming detail, virtuoso composition at its most spectacular.

Quiet study scene filling the square: the dealer examines the pages of a trade gazette with a large magnifying glass, following an undulating trend curve drawn among abstract wavy pencil scribbles (strictly no letters, no words, no typography — the printed matter is pure abstract squiggle texture); pencil behind his ear, a cup of coffee nearby, the plain desk and shelf behind extending to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and studious — pale cool morning light, sparse props, attentive beginner mood.

At his stall the dealer appraises a porcelain vase held up at eye level, turning it in the light with visible expertise; an open reference book on the counter shows small sketched plates of similar vases (pure sketch plates, no readable text), more wares and a second waiting customer filling the background to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly sharper than step 1 — warm light, richer surroundings, assured expert mood.

The legendary eye at work: crouched in a bustling flea-market aisle over a crate overflowing with bric-à-brac, the dealer holds a jeweler's loupe to his eye — and among the dense jumble one small teapot radiates an unmistakable warm golden glow that only he perceives; stalls, crates and rummaging silhouettes fill the scene to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: dramatic contrast between the golden find and the busy surroundings, treasure-hunt composition at its most spectacular.

Intimate corner of the stall filling the square: the dealer holds a delicate painted teacup in both hands and contemplates it with a soft, almost tender smile; two or three tiny hatched ink rays suggest a discreet warmth around the cup, simple shelves of modest wares behind him to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and gentle — pale soft morning light, sparse props, quiet affectionate mood.

The love grows: the dealer presents a gleaming mantel clock like a treasure on a small velvet cushion, eyes shining, chest slightly puffed with pride, the warm light of the scene gathering entirely on the beloved object; richer shelves and hanging pieces fill the background to the edges. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly warmer than step 1 — glowing presentation light, more cherished pieces around, proud devoted mood.

Head over heels: the dealer beams at the center of a joyful abundance of his favorite pieces — a framed painting hugged against his chest, a gramophone, vases, candlesticks and stacks of old books crowding affectionately around him like a court — fine radiating linework haloing the whole group, treasures filling every corner of the square to the edges. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: radiant golden light, overflowing abundance, exuberant composition at its most spectacular.

Tight duel over one object, the scene filling the square: the dealer and a customer lean face to face over a single earthenware jug placed exactly between them on the counter, the dealer's index finger delicately underlining the jug's handle mid-argument; the stall's shelves and hanging wares close the frame to the edges. STEP 1 of 3 in a visual progression: modest and restrained — pale cool light, sober gestures, focused opening-move mood.

Same duel, higher stakes: the dealer lifts the earthenware jug toward the light with a flourish to reveal its glaze — the jug catching the brightest highlight of the scene — one eyebrow raised, while the customer scratches his head, half-convinced; the surrounding stall busier, another curious onlooker at the edge of frame. STEP 2 of 3 in a visual progression: clearly livelier than step 1 — warm dramatic light on the object, more animation, persuasive mood.

Verbal gold: the joust concludes as the conquered customer pours a stream of coins into the dealer's open palm right above the earthenware jug, both men grinning, the jug sitting centered like a trophy on the counter with a warm aura; amused onlookers and the packed stall fill the scene to every edge. STEP 3 of 3, the apotheosis: golden celebratory light, dynamic exchange frozen mid-motion, the composition at its most spectacular.
